How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-16 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 CONFLUENCE flags tickers where 2+ coherent screeners align within one trading day, with validated multi-screener combos carrying demonstrated statistical edge in backtests.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes
ComboData confidenceBacktestRead

Any 3+ VALID screeners within ±1 dayFULL SIZEWR 81.7% · Sharpe 2.68 · p_bonf < 0.0001 (N=126, bucket-level)The bucket edge: triple agreement among validated screeners beats solos by +5.8pp WR.

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

UNH at 289 and ELV at 209 lead the batch, both catching CLENOW_MOMENTUM plus PULLBACK inside managed care, a momentum-with-dip setup in a sector with genuine catalyst flow. MS draws attention for breadth at a score of 184, with six screeners including BLOCK_TRADERS, PEAD_5D, and VARIANCE_PREMIUM firing simultaneously, but no validated combo matched for any name today, so none carry the SMART_FLOW x UNUSUAL_V3 or 3+ VALID screener lift that anchors the strongest backtest credentials. Twenty overlaps with the top two names pulling away from a field compressed between 99 and 184 reads as a narrow batch, not broad tape participation. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 provides no directional lift. Take UNH first at moderate conviction on the three-screener alignment and sector momentum setup, size light until a validated combo prints.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: HUM (score 5, target ~$349.18 (DD20d -9.7%)).

1 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.
Filter

All Screeners · ranked by backtest quality · type in the search box or click a chip to filter

🧠 Flags unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta direction, scored on premium size, signal persistence, and EMA 8/21 trend alignment.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $CZR $KDP $U
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1CZR ↑64 Watch$29.91 -0.73%BULLISH SWEEPCALL30$120KOPENING2567STRONG33
2KDP ↑50 Watch$31.38 +3.63%BULLISH SWEEPCALL31$227KOPENING248758STRONG22
3U ↑50 Watch$30.19 -3.45%BULLISH SWEEPCALL31$238KOPENING272858STRONG22
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape has only 3 resolved trades, making the 100% win rate and 200% average return versus a 3.48% SPY baseline statistically meaningless as confirmation of the backtest's 68.1% win rate, the verdict is WATCH, and until the resolved count clears 20 the monthly cohort streak of 100% wins across April, May, and June cannot be read as validation, the key number is n equals 3.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 273 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CZR leads at score 64, the only name with any separation from the Watch floor on a three-signal day. KDP and U both print 50, the minimum Watch threshold, both on BULLISH SWEEP patterns with no distinguishing factor between them. Zero signals reach 65, nothing clears 80, and with a median of 50 and a max of 64 the distribution is thin and compressed at the low end. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 adds no tailwind and argues against leaning hard into low-conviction Watch names. CZR is the first look if pressed, Watch tier only, sized accordingly.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape confirms the backtest, with a 90-day win rate of 80% and average gain of +3.8% versus SPY's -0.1% consistent with the 2.34 Sharpe, and the improving monthly cohort trend (April 50.8%, May 60.3%, June 81.4%) adds further confidence that the edge is current, not stale.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

KMB and PCG tie at the top at score 77, followed by CL, EXC, and PEP at 76, a batch dominated by staples and utilities that fits Sinclair's +31% structural premium edge for that sector. Pattern data is null across all names, so conviction rests entirely on the spread rank and sector classification. NEUTRAL macro at 54.96 neither lifts nor suppresses the setup, and the defensive character of today's names partly offsets that ambiguity. Start with PCG over KMB at moderate conviction, the utility classification amplifies the structural edge baked directly into the screener's construction.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

IONQ at 100 and HCA at 99 lead a strong cluster at the top of the batch, with JETS at 97 rounding out the three names worth examining first. Score distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 33 names print 80 or above against a median of 74, meaning genuine concentration of quality at the top rather than a flat, noisy wash. Pattern fields are null across all names, so ranking is driven purely by vol structure with no confirmatory price pattern to lean on. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 is not a headwind for this screener, which selects on options pricing coherence rather than directional macro tailwinds. IONQ first at high conviction on its perfect score, with HCA as a lower-beta complement if the quantum name feels too speculative for sizing.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is the story here: all 50 signals print exactly 100, median at 100, which makes this a binary pass/fail screen rather than a ranked one today. Among the top picks, ALL, BNY, and BBY each score 100 (Very strong, LONG), but pattern data is null across the board, removing any sub-signal for sequencing trades. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 is not a problem for the Kerberos thesis, which does not require a directional tape, but it adds no tailwind either. With no score gradient to exploit, preference goes to ALL first at moderate conviction, where deeper options markets make IV crush mechanics cleanest to execute.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d live tape contradicts the backtest directly - a 44% WR and -2.6% average return versus SPY flat is a hard KILL against the backtest's 65% WR and 1.10 Sharpe, though the monthly cohort trend offers a partial offset: March printed 67.3% WR and June 60.9%, suggesting the February collapse to 24.6% was regime-specific, and the Very strong tier's live 59.3% WR and +4.1% avg across 27 trades is the one number that keeps this screener on the watch list rather than the discard pile.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
STARTER ONLYWR 44% · avg -2.6% vs SPY -0.0% (-2.6 pts)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 489 resolved at +20d · WR 44% · avg -2.6% · vs SPY -0.0%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 495 closed · WR TB 29% · 17% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

MANU and PENN lead at perfect 100 scores in the Very strong tier with LAUNCH patterns, making them the two cleanest setups in today's batch. FENC, GBTG, HTLD, KNX, SGC, and SLG all sit at 95 with the same LAUNCH designation, giving a deep bench of high-conviction names. The score distribution is extreme - all 50 signals clear 80 and the median is 90, meaning there is no differentiation at the lower end and the screener is effectively outputting one compressed tier today. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 is neither a tailwind nor a veto for the long bias. Start with MANU or PENN at moderate conviction given their max scores and the Very strong tier's superior live track record, but size conservatively until the live tracking picture improves.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SMR at 92 leads the Very Strong tier with LUMN and SWKS both at 91, making that trio the most actionable cluster in today's batch. No pattern field is populated for any name, so the signal is purely score and tier driven, with no technical setup to layer on top. The distribution is healthy without being top-heavy: median 75, all 31 signals clear 65, and 8 reach 80 or above, giving the basket real depth beneath the top three. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 is an acceptable backdrop for oversold bounces but removes any risk-off tailwind that would sharpen the edge on this screener. Start with SMR given the highest score, sized as a basket entry at low conviction per name rather than a single-name trade, which is exactly what the backtest construction demands.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Flags vol-of-vol climax using VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity, and weekend effect to identify sell-vol setups at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑61 Watch$750.72 -0.54%SELL VOL1.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape confirms KEEP but with a narrow margin: 70% WR and +1.4% avg beat SPY by just 0.1 points, and while Signal-tier hits (9 trades, 88.9% WR, +2.35% avg) validate the logic, monthly cohort avg_20d has compressed from +5.4% in April to +0.57% in June, flagging shrinking magnitude even as hit rate holds.

🧠 Catches unusual options volume spikes filtered for calm, non-extended tickers to isolate informed institutional flow before a directional move.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The tape is too thin to confirm or contradict the backtest: only 15 of 620 signals have resolved, making the 100% win rate and 198.5% average 20-day return statistically void, the WATCH verdict holds until resolved count clears 20, and the backtest's 61.1% win rate over 72 trades remains the only usable reference - itself marginal at p-Bonferroni 0.097 with no DSR or rigorous audit behind it.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The dominant theme in today's 8-name batch is macro hedging: TLT scores highest at 70 (Signal, HEDGE), followed by SPY at 68 (Signal, BEARISH SWEEP) and KRE at 66 (Signal, BEARISH SWEEP), all three pointing to coordinated defensive positioning across bonds, broad equities, and regional banks simultaneously. Score distribution runs from 55 to 70 with a median of 55 and zero names clearing 80, placing this batch as moderate and top-compressed, not conviction-heavy. That defensive tilt fits inside a NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 - measured risk-off flow, not a panic signal. Take TLT first at low-to-moderate conviction: it carries the top score, the HEDGE pattern is clean, and bond protection layered against a SPY and KRE bearish sweep cluster is coherent positioning.

🧠 Detects sector momentum phase shifts by ranking ETF strength, acceleration, and RS crossover vs SPY to flag early entries and exhaustion.
Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLE and XLC both print 95 - Very strong - the only two names above the 80 threshold in an 11-sector universe where the median sits at 60, flagging a bifurcated distribution rather than broad participation. XLF registers 65 (Signal) and five remaining names cluster at 50 to 55 (Watch), confirming the batch is top-heavy but narrow. Every pattern field is null, stripping out the sub-category context the backtest most credits (DEAD, EARLY_ENTRY, NEW_DOWN) and limiting conviction materially. The macro regime reads NEUTRAL at 54.96, which does not confirm the energy and comms acceleration thesis. Treat XLE and XLC as co-equal first choices at reduced size, watchlist-only until pattern classification populates.

🧠 Identifies stocks down 40%-plus from their 52w high with capitulation volume and a reversal candle, targeting mean-reversion within 15 days.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking at 52% WR and +4.4% avg beats SPY by 4.0 points across 494 resolved signals and earns a KEEP verdict, but the drop from the backtest's 63% WR to 52% live is a meaningful decay - and the monthly cohort arc, from January's -12.9% avg to June's +5.4%, confirms this screener performs considerably better in recovering tapes than stressed ones.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LDOS (score 100, Very strong) and SOC (91, Very strong) are the only two names worth attention today, both printing BREAKOUT patterns at the top of a 50-signal batch where the median score is 77 and all 50 signals clear 65, making the distribution broad but not particularly elite. The remaining six names, ABTC, FIGS, HON, LVWR, SITC, and ARMP, all sit in the Strong tier, and live tracking shows that tier delivering only 37.8% WR and a negative avg return of -0.49%, which is a hard argument against acting on them. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.96, neither a tailwind nor a headwind for capitulation bounces, so the setup is acceptable but not ideal. Start with LDOS at moderate conviction given its perfect score and Very strong classification, hold SOC as a secondary idea, and pass on the Strong-tier names until the tape shows real follow-through.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks stocks by 90-day exponential-regression slope times R-squared, long only when price is above MA100 and SPY clears MA200.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

HUM is the only name worth flagging today, scoring 100 in the Very strong tier and standing alone at the top of an 18-signal list where the median score is 8. The next name, PANW at 62, sits in Watch tier, which has produced a 5% win rate and a -5.46% average 20-day return across 90 days of live tracking, making it noise rather than a trade. The distribution is brutally top-heavy: one signal above 65, one above 80, seventeen names below 65. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 is not the environment this screener is built for. Take HUM first and only, at moderate conviction, and pass the rest of this batch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Recent earnings with EPS surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Price ≥ $5, volume ≥ 500K, market cap ≥ $500M

Score
min(100, surprise_pct × 4). LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

GS prints a perfect 100 in the Very Strong tier and MS comes in at 72 Signal, both financials with no disclosed pattern data, so the setup thesis rests entirely on score and price momentum. The problem is live tracking inverts the tier logic: Very Strong runs a 30% win rate at -11.87% avg 20d, while the Watch tier runs 100% with a +9.23% avg. C at 62 sits in Watch, making it the only name whose tier has actually delivered in the live window. The batch is thin, four signals with a median of 66 and only one clearing 80, and the NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 provides no directional lift. First preference is C over GS despite the 38-point score gap, at low conviction only, with no position in GS until the tier inversion in live data resolves.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY long when two or more major intermarket pairs break their rolling correlation by 2 sigma over seven days, scaling severity with the break count.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single signal: SPY at score 70, Signal tier, LONG, pattern null. One point at 70 is the weakest qualifying output this screener can produce, sitting below the 80-plus range where the backtest doctrine claims its sharpest edge, and there is no pattern label to add texture. The score distribution is not clustered or broad, it is a single data point, which is a weak fire. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 adds no tailwind. If forced to act, minimum size only, and treat this as a monitor-not-trade setup until a score clears 80 or a second break confirms.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Detects post-earnings continuation in stocks with positive EPS surprise, gap-up open, and gap-hold into weeks 1-3, amplified by analyst revision momentum.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $AEHR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1AEHR ↑83●● Strong$84.20 -4.09%1566.7%215.0%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day resolved tape - WR 56%, avg +3.5% against SPY's +0.4% - technically earns a KEEP and outperforms the MARGINAL backtest label, but the June 2026 cohort's WR of 38% and avg of -0.69% directly contradicts that headline and signals the drift edge may be fading in the current environment; the key number is that -0.69% June mean, which is the worst monthly avg in the dataset.

Today's batch collapses to a single name: AEHR at score 83, Strong tier, PRIMED pattern, the only signal in the entire screen. Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CWAN at 93 is the only Very Strong name in today's batch and the clearest candidate, followed by PSNL at 89 and KURE and THG each at 88, all flagged LONG. Pattern context is null across all eight picks, so the signal rests entirely on premium weight, vol/OI ratio, and OI delta confirmation. The distribution is dense and elevated: all 50 signals clear the 65-point floor, 22 score at or above 80, and the median sits at 78, meaning the batch skews high but lacks meaningful separation below CWAN. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 adds no tailwind and removes no headwind. Take CWAN first at moderate conviction on tier alone, and treat PSNL and THG as secondary watches pending confirming tape.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.

All 50 signals today score 100 with zero spread, making score useless as a tiebreaker and the batch immediately suspect. Every pick lands in Very strong tier, which is the worst-performing bucket in live tracking at 45% win rate and -1.3% average 20-day return. Among the named names, AIG and DKS carry more liquidity and fundamental weight than ATNI or CTEV, but all four receive identical scores and no pattern data to differentiate them. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 provides no directional tailwind. Skip the batch entirely, or if forced to act, take AIG at low conviction only.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day resolved sample (495 trades, 61% WR, avg +5.0%) supports the KEEP verdict and confirms the screener beats SPY by 3.1 points, with monthly cohorts trending from April's near-washout at avg -0.73% back to June's +4.9%, but the key number is the tier inversion where Very strong prints 35.7% WR against Signal's 65% - a 29-point gap that makes the highest scores the worst bets in this dataset.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 61% · avg +5.0% · beats SPY +1.8% by +3.1 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 495 resolved at +20d · WR 61% · avg +5.0% · vs SPY +1.8%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 495 closed · WR TB 46% · 20% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BV (95) and DFH (90) lead on score but both sit in the Very strong tier, which is the worst historical performer at 35.7% win rate and avg -2.59%, making the two highest-scored names a trap. ACHC (85) and the five 80-score names (ABX, AKR, BCAX, BHVN, BMI) land in the Strong tier, marginally better at 53.5% WR but avg returns there are barely positive at +0.89%. Score distribution is top-heavy: 47 of 50 signals score 65 or above and 11 clear 80, with no meaningful mid-tier depth visible in today's picks. A NEUTRAL macro regime (54.96) offers no broad catalyst beyond stock-specific short dynamics. Preference is ACHC first at low conviction, avoiding BV and DFH unless the tier-performance inversion shown in the 90-day data reverses.

🧠 Ranks RUT-1000 stocks by relative volume times multi-horizon price momentum, then percentile-ranks the combined leader score 0-100.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is entirely ceiling-clustered: all 50 signals score 80 or above, median sits at 96, and eight names print 99 or 100, so the screener offers zero rank discrimination today. BTSG, NU, and PYPL each score 100 and CTAS, ABT, DINO, JBHT, and SEIC each score 99, but none carry a pattern flag, leaving the thesis thin beyond raw volume-momentum rank. A NEUTRAL macro regime (54.96) is neither tailwind nor headwind for a momentum setup, so the tape is not hostile, just unforgiving. PYPL is the most liquid of the perfect-scorers and the easiest to size without slippage, but given the live tracking data below, conviction here is low.

Data confidence STARTER prod-cabled KVO+FORCE combo, paper KEPT WR 67% vs SKIPPED 46% over 60d; no formal Bonferroni verdict yet Full methodology →
🧠 Filters institutional smart-flow signals by skipping entries where KVO or Force Index shows conflicting volume momentum within 3 days.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $CZR $KDP $U
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1CZR ↑64 Watch$29.91 -0.73%BULLISH SWEEPCALL30$120KOPENING2567STRONG33
2KDP ↑50 Watch$31.38 +3.63%BULLISH SWEEPCALL31$227KOPENING248758STRONG22
3U ↑50 Watch$30.19 -3.45%BULLISH SWEEPCALL31$238KOPENING272858STRONG22
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

CZR leads at a score of 64 with a BULLISH SWEEP pattern, the only name that clears the field, while KDP and U both land at the median of 50 with identical patterns and no differentiation. Three signals, nothing at or above 65, max score stuck at 64 - this is a thin, low-conviction batch. NEUTRAL macro at 54.96 does not provide any directional tailwind to lean on. If forced to act, CZR first at minimum size only, with no urgency to chase KDP or U at a score that sits exactly at the batch median.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects Market Profile rejection tails at value-area extremes (bottom 30%, top 70%) exceeding 1.8x ATR, the only configuration that backtested positive on mean reversion.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $RPHS
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1RPHS ↑90●●● Very strong$8.79 -0.28%LOW1.8126.8%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 8-trade live record shows 62.5% directional accuracy and 1.74% average 20d return vs 0.04% for SPY, which looks supportive but falls well short of the 20-trade minimum needed to move off WATCH, and the options book at 27% WR with 27% expiry rate - the key number - directly contradicts the directional signal's apparent edge.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

RPHS is the only name today, scoring 90 (Very strong, LONG), but the pattern field returns null, which limits visibility into the specific tail structure behind the signal. A single-name batch is weak on volume regardless of score quality. The NEUTRAL macro regime (54.96) is not a tailwind for reversal setups but not a hard headwind either. If you trade one name from a batch this thin, size it at half normal conviction and treat RPHS as a watch first, trigger second.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) in the last 3 bars
  • SHORT direction: excess on the HIGH side AND trade location ≥ 70%
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). SHORT.

🧠 Targets post-earnings drift continuation: buy the weekly close above prior-week high after a red-candle pullback within four weeks of an earnings gap-up.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape directly confirms the KILL verdict: 90d WR of 36.8% and avg of -7.1% run 6.3 points behind SPY, while June 2026 cohort WR of 30.7% and avg of -7.5% show conditions worsening rather than mean-reverting toward the backtest's 52.7% win rate.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

DFTX is the only Strong-tier name today, scoring 80 with a TRIGGER pattern, the one genuine setup in a batch where 49 of 50 signals sit below the Strong threshold. APGE and CRNX each score 70 in Signal tier with SOW ZONE patterns, showing parabolic structure but not full climax exhaustion. The distribution is broad and thin at the top: median 60 across 50 signals, just one at 80 and only 16 at 65 or above, making this batch diluted. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 offers no directional tailwind for shorts, and the screener carries a REJECTED backtest and a live KILL verdict. Start with DFTX at very low conviction, sizing small, and pass on the Signal tier entirely until this screener shows a cleaner record.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Every name in today's batch scores 100 with no pattern context attached, so AMD, DELL, and MSFT are the only candidates worth naming on liquidity and size grounds alone. The score distribution is not a distribution - median 99, all 50 signals at or above 80 - the model has collapsed its entire output into one undifferentiated cluster, which is a signal about the signal, not about the stocks. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (54.96), offering no directional tailwind for a momentum model that is already broken in live trading. The backtest was rejected before this batch was generated: alpha is -0.8%/yr at p=0.25, the headline return was a leveraged-ETF artifact, and the data source itself flags paper-only, do-not-size. No name from this screener is actionable today at any conviction.

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven validity test Sharpe 0.98 N=8 CI extreme, doctrine-rejected solo Full methodology →
🧠 Weekly RSI(14) below 30 gates entry, then daily oversold crossovers, candlestick reversals, and zone scoring identify contrarian long setups.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $TDS $CAPR $PSIX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSI (D)RSI (W)CrossoverPattern
1TDS ↑58 Watch$34.35 +0.79%31.2218.84YESENGULFING
2CAPR ↑57 Watch$19.12 -6.04%6.5329.80NOHAMMER
3PSIX ↑52 Watch$31.76 -5.08%28.4929.36T-1HAMMER
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape on 496 resolved trades (54% WR, avg +4.2%, beating SPY by 3.3 pts) shows a real edge that the doctrine-rejected backtest (N=8) could never establish, but monthly cohorts tell a fading story - win rate has slid from 78.8% in March to 52.6% in June, and May's average return turned negative at -0.44%, which is the key number to watch.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is thin: three signals, all Watch tier, scores compressed between 52 and 58 with nothing clearing 65 and nothing in the Strong or Signal tier. TDS at 58 is the top name, CAPR at 57 is a hair behind, and PSIX at 52 is a filler entry with no distinguishing characteristic beyond passing the weekly gate. The score distribution is bottom-heavy and the pattern label on all three is WATCHLIST, meaning no confirmed intraday trigger has fired. In a NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54.96) there is no environmental lift to compensate for the low conviction. TDS first at half-size if anything, but the honest read is to wait for a day with Signal-tier entries or scores above 65.

Data confidence STARTER · edge unproven mega-session 2026-05-16 crypto battery: 0/16 signals survive adversarial validation post-spot-ETF 2024 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional positioning in crypto-linked ETFs via volume spikes, OI delta, and premium deviation, aiming to front-run spot crypto moves.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.
Top picks: $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayRSIRS IBIT 20dRegime
1MSTR ↑55 Watch$94.03 -3.53%45.34-22.8%risk_on
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the backtest REJECT directly: 37% win rate, average return -6.9% against SPY +0.3%, and June 2026 went 0-for-51 with an average loss of 18.5%, showing the edge collapsed once spot ETF flows normalized post-2024.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

HACK leads the batch at 71 - the only ticker clearing 70 - with BUG also scoring 67, making cybersecurity the standout theme pair today. ARKG and IBB both come in at 67, clustering biotech and genomics exposure right behind cyber, though neither carries a pattern signal to anchor an entry. The score distribution is low-ceiling and compressed: zero names above 80, only 11 at or above 65, and a median of 57, which reads as a thin and uninspired sweep. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 does not argue for pressing high-beta theme exposure, particularly genomics names like ARKG that are most sensitive to rate sentiment. Take HACK first at low conviction - it leads the batch, cybersecurity demand is structural rather than purely momentum-driven, and the BUG confirmation adds mild thematic weight.

🧠 The 90-day live record at 65% WR and +7.9% avg beats SPY by 8.8 points and earns a KEEP, but with no formal backtest to anchor against and only one monthly cohort available (May 2026, WR 66%, avg 43% - the latter heavily distorted by a single 1385% outlier), the most important internal signal is a tier inversion: Watch names (n=91) averaged +10.0% vs Very Strong names (n=11) at +4.7%, running directly opposite to what the tier labels imply and unresolved without a rigorous backtest.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 65% · avg +7.9% · beats SPY -0.9% by +8.8 pts
Last 90 days: 136 signals captured · 131 resolved at +20d · WR 65% · avg +7.9% · vs SPY -0.9%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 37% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight tickers score 100 today, all Very Strong tier: BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM. SRTY is the most anomalous name - a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF appearing as a LONG signal alongside single-stock longs, which either reflects small-cap weakness becoming the dominant theme or a screener construction artifact worth auditing before trading it. CELH at 100 is the cleaner entry, with an identifiable momentum recovery thesis and no structural contradiction in the signal. Score distribution is top-heavy: 8 of 50 signals sit at the absolute ceiling, median is 70, and 41 of 50 clear 65, so the batch compresses high with little gradation across names. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.96, no directional lift, and pattern data is null across every pick, which caps conviction; start with CELH at moderate size and hold off on SRTY until the ETF inclusion logic is confirmed.

🧠 Aggregates quarterly 13F filings from 20 hedge funds, scoring stocks on new positions and size increases to surface concentrated institutional conviction.
Construction
Quarterly SEC 13F-HR filings from 20 top hedge funds via EDGAR. Whale Score: +20/NEW (cap 60), +10/INCREASED (cap 30), -5/CLOSED, -3/DECREASED. Stale 60-90 days by nature (quarterly).

The score distribution is fully compressed: all 50 signals print at exactly 100, the median is 100, and every name clears both the 65 and 80 thresholds, which means the screener provides zero ranking differentiation across today's batch. META and ASML lead the list by name recognition and liquidity alone, not by any screener edge, since the data assigns them identical scores to MKSI and ENS. Pattern data is null across all picks, stripping away any technical overlay that might have added resolution. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 offers no directional tailwind to lean into this batch with conviction. If forced to sequence, META is the first call by institutional breadth and options market depth, but this batch is mechanically weak for any kind of ranked actionability.

FINS leads at a perfect score of 100, followed by BGDE at 99 and the NTSK/BOT pair at 98 - all rated Very strong - but pattern metadata is null for every name, removing the context needed to judge what drove the buying. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54.96) is neither a tailwind nor a headwind for insider-buy signals, so the regime does not sharpen the case. FINS gets the first look on score alone, at low conviction, pending confirmation of officer rank, trade size relative to compensation, and whether multiple insiders clustered in the same window.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks non-financial, non-utility equities above $200M market cap by EV/EBIT ascending, surfacing the cheapest operating earners as potential mean-reversion longs.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

14CRMD ↑57 Watch$8.29 -1.54%4.33$0.65B$0.15B$0.66Byfinance
15DXC ↑53 Watch$9.39 +0.97%4.42$4.29B$0.97B$1.53Byfinance

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no backtest to compare against, the 90-day tape stands alone and it is not encouraging: 5 resolved trades show a 20% win rate and a mean 20-day return of -0.06% against SPY's 1.36% baseline, with June 2026 the only monthly cohort on record - the WATCH verdict is correct and the key number is 5, which is one quarter of the 20 minimum needed to say anything with confidence.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

BZ scores 100 and WRD scores 97, both Very strong tier - the two cheapest names in today's universe by the multiple, though neither carries a pattern signal to sharpen the entry. KROS at 93 rounds out the top three Very strong names, giving the batch four consecutive Very strong readings before stepping down to Strong at GPC (87). The distribution is top-heavy: 7 of 16 signals print at 80 or above and the median sits at 73, meaning the cheap end of the universe is genuinely bunched rather than a thin trickle. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 54.96) neither supports nor kills a deep value tilt today. BZ is the first call by score alone, sized small, because there is no backtest to lean on and the live tape has not confirmed any edge yet.

🧠 Finds individual stocks lagging within sectors already in LATE_CHASE or RIDING phase, betting sector momentum eventually pulls stragglers up.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is weak and should be treated as such. All 16 signals score exactly 50, the median equals the max, and zero names clear 65, meaning the scoring engine found no real differentiation. SBAC and CCI are the most recognizable names, both cell tower REITs that can catch bids on rate-expectation shifts, but neither carries a pattern flag and both sit at Watch tier only. STZ and CSGP are the next most liquid, with the same flat read. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 54.96 provides no catch-up tailwind, and the screener's catch-up thesis historically wants a risk-on tape. If forced to prioritize, SBAC first given REIT sector sensitivity, but conviction is low enough that skipping the batch is the cleaner call.

🧠 Detects SEC Form 4 cluster buys of 3+ insiders within 30 days, weighting executive rank and recency to separate conviction from routine filings.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape (63% WR, +5.7% avg, beating SPY by 6.1 points) looks clean but no formal backtest exists to confirm the edge, and April's +18.9% cohort average is inflated by a single 539% outlier - May and June settle to +4.9% and +6.3%, which is the more representative baseline.

🧠 Ranks tickers by 20-day relative strength versus SPY within bullish sectors, using sector breadth and cluster position to isolate leaders likely to sustain outperformance.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $ABX $ACA $ACAD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1ABX ↑80●● Strong$10.51 -3.40%Financials18.7%30.6%
2ACA ↑68● Signal$145.00 +0.10%Industrials12.2%27.2%
3ACAD ↑64 Watch$25.63 -1.12%Healthcare18.9%13.9%
4ABVX ↑58 Watch$137.77 +0.61%Healthcare40.6%7.9%
5ACCO ↑57 Watch$4.18 +5.29%Industrials-2.5%25.5%
6AAMI ↑55 Watch$83.27 +0.24%Financials0.8%21.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags retail-euphoria candidates (call volume spike, social mention surge, parabolic price, no catalyst) for short positioning against mean reversion.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $AA $ASTS $ASX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1AA ↓100●●● Very strong$46.85 -3.56%FOMO2.47-3.6%
2ASTS ↓100●●● Very strong$55.01 -17.04%FOMO1.01-17.0%
3ASX ↓100●●● Very strong$39.51 -2.97%FOMO4.09-3.0%
4AMKR ↓51 Watch$63.02 -6.83%GREED0.51-6.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where 30-day implied vol exceeds 20-day realized vol by more than 1.5 standard deviations, surfacing overstated fear premiums for sale.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $BMRN $LLY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1BMRN ↓62 Watch$60.00 +1.78%42%27%1.56FEAR
2LLY ↓62 Watch$1169.17 +1.08%51%33%1.55FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape returns a 43% win rate and average gain of +1.1% matching SPY +1.1% tick-for-tick, confirming no premium-selling edge exists yet, and monthly cohorts are deteriorating in the wrong direction - the key number is June's 33% win rate paired with a -3.65% average, the worst rolling month in the series, which makes the NOT_YET_BACKTESTED status read as a warning rather than a placeholder.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects fake breakdowns below VAL or fake breakouts above VAH that reverse next session, trapping momentum players on the wrong side.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $ADNT $AZO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1ADNT ↑55 Watch$20.61 +3.41%BEAR_TRAP19.6522.240.93
2AZO ↑55 Watch$3062.16 +3.08%BEAR_TRAP2963.533080.4397.86
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no formal backtest on record, the 90-day tape's 67% win rate and +5.8% avg across 58 resolved trades is the only evidence base, and the cohort sequence from May (77% WR, +8.0% avg) to June (65% WR, +4.5% avg) confirms the mechanism is producing real edge but the key number - +5.7 points of excess return over SPY - is being earned on a softening trend worth watching.

Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.

1SVXY ↓85●● Strong$58.12 -1.21%28%720%RULE_1

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects extreme put-IV dominance over call-IV at the 90th-plus percentile, flagging unusual demand for downside protection.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$374.45 -5.03%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags names where straddle IV sits below Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, signaling the market is underpricing pre-earnings realized vol.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $HON
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1HON ↑65● Signal$226.33 +1.57%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

HON is the sole signal at a score of 65, the floor of the Signal tier, with direction LONG and no pattern data recorded. One name at the minimum threshold is a weak batch by any measure. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 54.96, which neither supports nor suppresses vol-buying setups. If you trade this at all, HON straddles are the only option, sized well below normal given missing pattern context and the absence of any high-conviction companion names.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put/call skew spikes above the 90-day baseline as contrarian long signals when the underlying is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $MSTR
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1MSTR ↑55 Watch$94.03 -3.53%66%960.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape logs 55% WR and +6.7% average, beating SPY by 5.4 points, which sounds like confirmation - but the tier breakdown contradicts the backtest framing entirely: the 'Very strong' and 'Strong' tiers post negative average returns of -5.6% and -4.1% at sub-30% win rates, while Watch tier at the low end of the score range dominates at +13.4%, meaning the screener's edge inverts at high signal intensity and the key number is 68.75% - the Watch win rate, not the headline.

Altman

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 Screens US equities using Altman Z-score derived metrics to identify financial distress risk, with edge rooted in credit dislocation ahead of market repricing.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is empty. One signal fired and scored zero, placing it below every meaningful threshold - no name clears 65 or 80. There are no top picks to rank. The score distribution is effectively a null event: one observation, no signal strength. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL with a 54.96 score, which is neither a headwind nor a tailwind for distress-oriented screens. Conviction is zero today; pass on the batch entirely.

🧠 STFR is a proprietary screener; construction logic is not exposed in the available data, so the detection mechanism cannot be characterized.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags supply and demand imbalances by reading the relationship between price spread, volume, and bar close position.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
